note: Master parts catalogue. Every component exists once. Projects reference these by partId. Prices in INR.

P0001.name: ESP32 DevKit (WROOM-32, 30-pin)
P0001.alternatives: Any 30-pin ESP32 WROOM DevKit works as long as GPIO16/17 are broken out. ESP32-WROVER also fine.

P0002.name: Mini560 5V fixed buck
P0002.alternatives: LM2596 adjustable (₹45) — but you MUST set the pot to exactly 5.0V with a multimeter and lock it (nail polish) before connecting. Mini560 is fixed 5V so safer.
P0002.notes: Powers the ESP32 from the AC 14V rail.

P0003.name: HLK-B0505S-2WR3 isolated DC-DC
P0003.alternatives: B0505S-1WR3 (1W) also fine — the hot side draws little. Any 5V-to-isolated-5V 1-2W brick works.
P0003.notes: Provides the isolated 5V for the hot side of the S403 interface.

P0004.name: ADuM1201ARZ digital isolator
P0004.alternatives: 2x 6N139 optocouplers (₹42 each, ₹84 total) replace it and are DIP-8 so you can DROP the SOIC adapter. But it changes the circuit: add 2 LED resistors (~1k) + 2 pull-ups (~4.7k) and set inverted:true on both UART pins in ESPHome. Cheaper PC817 2-ch module (₹56) works too but slower edges.
P0004.notes: Heart of the S403 interface — the isolation barrier. Only robu stocks the ADuM. This is the one part you must not skimp quality on.

P0005.name: SOIC8 to DIP8 adapter PCB
P0005.alternatives: Only needed if you use the SMD ADuM1201. If you go the 6N139 opto route (DIP-8) you do not need this at all.
P0005.notes: Lets the SMD ADuM sit on the perfboard.

P0006.name: SMBJ18A TVS diode
P0006.alternatives: SMBJ17A (17V standoff, ₹5) is fine for the 14V rail. Any 16-20V unidirectional TVS in DO-214 works.

P0008.name: RXEF020 200mA polyfuse (PTC)
P0008.alternatives: 500mA glass fuse + 5x20 holder (~₹15) works but is NOT resettable — replace if it blows, and it trips coarser (500mA vs 200mA). Any 200-250mA PTC is a drop-in.
P0008.notes: Guards the AC 14V rail against a dead short.

P0009.name: 470uF / 16V electrolytic (105C)
P0009.notes: Bulk cap on the 5V rails. Need 2.

P0010.name: 100nF MLCC ceramic
P0010.alternatives: Any 100nF (0.1uF) ceramic, SMD or through-hole. Extremely common — grab a strip.

P0011.name: 1k ohm 1/4W resistor
P0011.alternatives: Carbon or metal film both fine. 1k is the most common value — any pack has it.

P0012.name: 3mm LED (red + green)
P0012.alternatives: Any 3mm or 5mm indicator LED, any colour. Use with ~1k resistor at 5V.

P0013.name: 2.54mm 1x40 male header strip
P0013.alternatives: Universal — any 2.54mm male berg strip, snap to length.
P0013.notes: For mounting the ESP32 on the perfboard.

P0014.name: JST/CJT 10-pin 2.5mm harness (A2508H)
P0014.alternatives: NO good substitute — Quartz only stocks XH up to 5-pin, and two housings side-by-side break the 2.54 pitch. Shaved-down female Dupont is a last-resort hack (less secure). Buy the real robu part.
P0014.notes: The S403 connector. This is the one part that MUST come from robu — order it even as a separate small shipment.

P0015.name: Prototype PCB, FR-4
P0015.alternatives: Quartz bakelite board (₹24) is cheaper but BRITTLE — it can crack when you cut the isolation slot. Any FR-4 perfboard >= 7x9cm is better.
P0015.notes: Board that hosts the whole interface. FR-4 preferred for the isolation slot.

P0016.name: IP65 ABS junction box
P0016.alternatives: OPTIONAL — the module lives inside the AC housing anyway, so any sturdy plastic box (or none) works. Do not buy unless you specifically want a sealed enclosure.

P0017.name: VS1838B IR receiver
P0017.alternatives: TSOP1838 / TSOP4838 / any 38kHz IR receiver head is a drop-in.
P0017.notes: For learning the Orient Zeno fan remote codes.

P0018.name: 38kHz IR transmitter module
P0018.alternatives: Discrete 940nm IR LED (₹4) + 2N2222 driver (₹3) = ₹7 and gives longer range (the guide max-range circuit). Slightly more soldering.
P0018.notes: Blasts commands to the fan. The discrete version is actually better range.

P0019.name: 2N2222 NPN transistor
P0019.alternatives: BC547, S8050, 2N3904 — any small-signal NPN works as the IR LED driver.
P0019.notes: IR LED driver for the max-range circuit. Need 2.

P0020.name: AM312 mini PIR sensor
P0020.alternatives: MH-SR602 (~₹50) is a smaller drop-in with adjustable sensitivity. Standard HC-SR501 (~₹60) also works but is bigger and draws more. Avoid branded SmartElex AM312 — same sensor at ₹189.
P0020.notes: Generic AM312 ₹59. SmartElex jumped to ₹189 for the identical sensor.

P0021.name: TP4056 charger WITH protection
P0021.alternatives: MUST be the 6-pin PROTECTED version (DW01+FS8205 chips) — the 4-pin bare TP4056 has no protection and will over-discharge your salvaged cells. Type-C protected (₹13) is cheaper but often out of stock (join waitlist); mini-USB protected is electrically identical.
P0021.notes: Non-negotiable: protected version only. Charges + protects the 18650.

P0022.name: AO3400 N-channel MOSFET
P0022.alternatives: Any logic-level N-MOSFET: AO3402, SI2302, or through-hole 2N7000 (if you can find TO-92 stock). Must be fully on at <=2.5V gate.
P0022.notes: Low-side switch. SHARED between Night Light (6) and Presence (1) — buy a strip of 10.

P0023.name: BC547 NPN transistor
P0023.alternatives: BC548, 2N3904, S8050 — any general small-signal NPN.
P0023.notes: Daylight-clamp transistor with the LDR. Sold in packs of 20; you need ~6 pieces, rest to inventory.

P0024.name: GL5516 LDR (5-10k)
P0024.alternatives: GL5528, GL5537 — any 5mm LDR works, just re-tune the trimmer.
P0024.notes: SHIELD it from the units own LEDs or you get a flicker loop. Need ~6.

P0025.name: Warm-white 5mm LED
P0025.alternatives: Any warm-white 5mm LED. robu is out of warm (only pre-wired cool packs ₹44/5). Buy warm loose at any local shop for ₹1-2 — saves a lot.
P0025.notes: Buy local. Need ~18 (3 per unit x 6).

P0026.name: RM065 100k trimmer preset
P0026.alternatives: Any 100k multi-turn or single-turn trimmer. 50k-200k all usable.
P0026.notes: Hold-time / threshold adjust. Need ~6, sold in packs of 10.

P0027.name: SPDT slide switch
P0027.alternatives: Any small SPDT/SPST slide or toggle switch.
P0027.notes: Per-unit master off. Need ~6, sold in packs of 5.

P0028.name: Perfboard 2x8cm
P0028.alternatives: Any small perfboard, cut to size. A 10x15cm board cut into 6 is cheaper.

P0029.name: JST XH 2-pin plug+socket
P0029.alternatives: Any 2-pin polarised connector (JST XH/PH, or even a barrel jack) for the modular battery pack.
P0029.notes: Makes battery packs swappable (18650 or 3xAA). Need ~6 pairs.

P0030.name: Assorted 1/4W resistors (68R,1k,47k,100k)
P0030.alternatives: Buy per-value packs of 10 for each value you need. Or a 100-piece assortment kit (~₹100) covers these + future builds.
P0030.notes: 68R for dual-voltage LED compatibility, plus bias resistors. Roughly 30-40 pieces across values.

P0031.name: 18650 cell
P0031.alternatives: Buy new 18650 (~₹150) or a 3xAA holder if you have no salvage cells. Never charge alkaline AAs.
P0031.notes: FREE from old laptop packs / power banks. Vet >=2.8V, bin <2.0V. Need ~6.

P0032.name: ESP32 DevKit
P0032.alternatives: Same as the Daikin ESP32 — any 30-pin WROOM DevKit.
P0032.notes: Second ESP32, runs the LD2410C + relay board in the false ceiling.

P0033.name: HLK-LD2410C mmWave presence sensor
P0033.alternatives: LD2410B (bare) is the same sensor. LD2450 (₹600-800) only pays off if you later want zone/position logic — same wiring, swap later.
P0033.notes: Native ESPHome, all sensitivity gates exposed in HA. Faces down through the false-ceiling tile.

P0034.name: 2-channel 5V relay board
P0034.alternatives: Any 2-ch 5V relay board with opto isolation. Or 2x Sonoff Mini R4 (flashed ESPHome, ~₹1200) if you prefer certified modules.
P0034.notes: Switches both bulb groups, wired in series AFTER the wall switches (parked ON).

P0035.name: HLK-PM05 (230VAC to 5V 1A)
P0035.alternatives: Old 5V phone-charger PCB (₹0) is an acceptable salvage fallback, just bulkier. Any small 5V 1A+ AC-DC module works.
P0035.notes: Powers the whole ceiling node from the light-point switched-live + neutral.

P0036.name: ESP32-C3 Super Mini (desk node)
P0036.alternatives: Any small ESP32-C3 / ESP32-S2 board. Even a full ESP32 works if it fits.
P0036.notes: Cuts USB power to the BT LED strip (which auto-resumes on power) via the AO3400.

P0037.name: 1A fuse + holder
P0037.alternatives: Any 1A slow-blow mains fuse + inline holder.
P0037.notes: Mains-side protection at the ceiling node.

P0038.name: Junction box (ceiling node)
P0038.alternatives: Any sturdy box that fits above the false ceiling. Salvage is fine.

P0039.name: USB male-female pigtail
P0039.alternatives: Any short USB extension you can cut — the MOSFET goes inline on the +5V (or GND) wire.
P0039.notes: The MOSFET switches the strips USB power here.
